generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retrieve and refresh all Event and Activity data from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 on a fixed 60-second cycle without requiring user intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates a fixed 60-second automated refresh cycle as the primary mechanism for real-time situational awareness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a persistent, visually prominent indicator showing the timestamp of the last successful data refresh from each source system (Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0) independently, so that controllers are never silently operating on stale data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation pattern requires explicit staleness signaling to prevent controllers from mistaking outdated data for live data during high-tempo exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: When data retrieval from one or both source systems fails or returns no response within the 60-second refresh window, the system shall display a clearly visible error banner identifying the affected source system(s), the time of last successful refresh, and a degraded-data warning, while continuing to display the most recently retrieved data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation must prevent silent data failures; controllers must be informed of source system unavailability without losing the last known good display state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a Multi-Document Interface (MDI) layout that permanently allocates the left two-thirds of the application window to a graphical timeline view and the right one-third to a data table region. This layout ratio shall be fixed and not user-configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 defines a fixed, non-configurable screen proportion that governs the entire display architecture.

generated_requirements.text: Within the data table region, the top one-quarter shall display sequential Events (order-dependent, not time-bound) and the bottom three-quarters shall display temporally relevant Events ordered by proximity to current exercise time. This segmentation ratio shall be fixed.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 defines the fixed segmentation of the table region into sequential and temporal sub-panels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall classify Events into the sequential or temporal table segment based on a designated field or attribute present in the MSEL Summary data as provided by the source applications. When an Event's classification field is absent, null, or ambiguous, the system shall place the Event in the temporal segment and display a visual indicator flagging the classification anomaly.
generated_requirements.rationale: The sequential/temporal boundary must be driven by source data fields per A-08, and edge cases for missing or ambiguous classification must be explicitly handled.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code each displayed Event and Activity based on its variance from current exercise time according to the following thresholds, which shall conform to the DHS color schema specification: (a) on-time or not yet due within a defined early-warning window — green; (b) within a defined at-risk variance window past scheduled time — yellow; (c) beyond the at-risk window and overdue — red; (d) completed — a distinct neutral DHS-compliant color. Exact minute thresholds for each band shall be defined and documented during requirements elaboration with DHS stakeholders.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 mandates color-coded variance indicators; specific threshold bands must be defined to make the requirement testable and compliant with DHS color schema.

generated_requirements.text: All visual elements of the application — including but not limited to backgrounds, text, table row highlights, chart fills, status indicators, clock displays, error banners, and filter controls — shall conform to the official DHS color schema specification document. No color values outside the approved schema shall be used without a formal DHS waiver.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-01 is a hard regulatory constraint requiring full DHS color schema compliance across every UI element.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display two independent, simultaneously visible clocks at all times: one showing current local (wall-clock) time and one showing current exercise time. The two clocks shall be visually distinct in label and style, and both shall be visible in both projection mode and workstation mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires dual-clock display to eliminate confusion between local and exercise time during execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall obtain exercise time from a designated authoritative source (e.g., a dedicated exercise clock service or EMT 1.0). The authoritative source for exercise time shall be specified in the system configuration. When the exercise time source is unavailable, the system shall freeze the exercise clock at the last known value, display a prominent warning indicating that exercise time is unconfirmed, and continue displaying local time from the system clock.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exercise time sourcing and failure behavior must be explicitly specified to prevent incorrect variance calculations and controller confusion.

generated_requirements.text: When exercise time is manually adjusted by an authorized user or updated by the authoritative source, the system shall immediately recalculate and re-render all color-coded variance indicators and temporal Event ordering on the next display refresh cycle, and shall log the time adjustment event in the history report.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exercise time adjustments directly affect all variance calculations and must be reflected immediately and audited.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a Pause control that suspends the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle. While paused, the system shall display a persistent, high-visibility 'DISPLAY PAUSED' indicator showing the exact date and time the pause was initiated and the elapsed time since the last successful refresh, so that the age of displayed data is unambiguous at all times.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pause/resume state must be visually unambiguous to prevent controllers from mistaking a frozen display for a live one, a critical failure mode in operations center environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a Resume control that restarts the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle from the moment of resumption. Upon resumption, the system shall immediately trigger a data refresh and clear the paused-state indicator.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 requires that the 60-second cycle resume on user command; immediate refresh on resume ensures data currency is restored without waiting a full cycle.

generated_requirements.text: Active filter states (venue filter, key event filter, and any combination thereof) shall persist across pause/resume cycles and across automatic refresh cycles. Filter states shall not persist across application restarts unless the user explicitly saves a named filter configuration. A 'Clear All Filters' control shall be prominently available at all times.
generated_requirements.rationale: Filter state persistence and reset behavior must be explicitly defined to prevent unintended data loss or confusion during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Venue and by Key Events designation. Filters shall be applicable independently or in combination (logical AND). When filters are active, the system shall display a visible indicator identifying which filters are currently applied.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates filtering by at minimum two dimensions; combined filter support and active-filter visibility reduce cognitive load during execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an on-demand 'Full Exercise View' control that instantly removes all active filters and displays all Events and Activities in the exercise without requiring the user to manually clear individual filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 mandates an unfiltered full-exercise view accessible on demand, regardless of active filter state.

generated_requirements.text: Each displayed Event and Activity shall include a direct navigation link (deep link) that, when activated, opens or brings focus to the corresponding record in the originating source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0). The deep-link mechanism shall use the documented, stable interface of the target application (e.g., URI scheme, COM automation, or named pipe as specified in the integration design). EEMDS shall never write to or modify any data in either source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires deep-link navigation to source applications as the sole modification pathway; the mechanism must be specified and EEMDS must remain strictly read-only.

generated_requirements.text: When a deep-link navigation is activated and the target source application is not running, the system shall display an informational dialog notifying the user that the target application must be launched before navigation can proceed, and shall not attempt to modify any data. When the target record is not found in the source application, the system shall display an error message identifying the missing record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deep-link failure modes must be handled gracefully to prevent user confusion and ensure the read-only constraint is never violated.

generated_requirements.text: All UI controls that display Event or Activity data fields shall be rendered as read-only, non-editable elements. No inline text editing, drag-and-drop reordering, or any other affordance that could be interpreted as a data editing capability shall be present in the EEMDS interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and the read-only scope constraint require that EEMDS provide zero data editing capability; UI design must make this unambiguous.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display only MSEL Summary data fields for each Event and Activity in both the graphical timeline and the data table regions. Full MSEL detail records shall not be displayed within EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 restricts the data scope to MSEL Summary data only, which also limits data exposure for sensitivity and classification reasons.

generated_requirements.text: Authorized users shall be able to define, modify, and save named time segments used to organize Events on the graphical timeline. Time segment configuration shall be accessible prior to and during exercise execution. The system shall require at least one time segment to be defined before the timeline display is rendered.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires user-configurable time segments; the pre-condition of at least one segment prevents an undefined timeline state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture a history record for every change to Event or Activity data that is detected between successive refresh cycles from either source system. Each history record shall include at minimum: (a) timestamp in local time, (b) timestamp in exercise time, (c) source system identifier (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0), (d) Event or Activity identifier, (e) changed field name, (f) previous field value, (g) new field value, and (h) user context if available from the source system.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 mandates a complete audit history; field-level delta capture with dual timestamps and source attribution is required for AAR completeness and HSEEP compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The history report shall be retrievable at any point during or after the exercise via a dedicated report interface within EEMDS. The report shall be exportable in at least one structured format suitable for AAR use (e.g., CSV or PDF). The report shall support filtering by date/time range, source system, and Event or Activity identifier.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 requires history report availability during and after exercise; export and filtering capabilities are necessary for AAR analyst workflows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate actual versus assumed time variance charts for Events and Activities, displaying the delta between the scheduled (assumed) time and the recorded actual completion or execution time. These charts shall be accessible on demand from within the EEMDS interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD future state and scope explicitly include actual vs. assumed time variance charts as a quantitative analysis capability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control. Only users pre-authorized by the system administrator shall be granted access to the EEMDS application. The system shall positively authenticate each user at login and deny access to any unrecognized or unauthorized user. No guest, anonymous, or shared-credential access modes shall be supported.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and RC-02 mandate positive authentication and zero unauthorized access; anonymous modes are explicitly prohibited.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support at minimum two display rendering profiles: (a) Projection Mode — optimized for viewing at distances of 30 to 100 feet on a 10×10 projection screen, with minimum font size of 24pt for primary data fields, high-contrast DHS-compliant color rendering, and enlarged status indicators; and (b) Workstation Mode — optimized for single-user desktop viewing at standard monitor distances. The active rendering profile shall be selectable by the user and shall be visually labeled in the interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD and domain expert advisory require explicit projection and workstation rendering profiles to meet readability standards at operational distances.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 exclusively through their documented, stable, versioned data interfaces (e.g., database read connection, published API, or file export). The specific interface type and version for each source system shall be recorded in the system configuration and integration design documentation. EEMDS shall not use undocumented or internal interfaces of either source system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Source system interface stability is a critical constraint; undocumented interface use creates fragile integrations that break silently during source system updates.

generated_requirements.text: Each EEMDS session shall be independent of other concurrently running EEMDS sessions on other client PCs or display outputs. Pausing, resuming, or changing filter state on one session shall have no effect on any other session. Each session shall maintain its own refresh cycle, filter state, and display configuration.
generated_requirements.rationale: In a multi-display operations center, session independence prevents a single controller action from inadvertently affecting shared displays.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce an inactivity session timeout consistent with applicable DHS IT security policy. Upon timeout, the session shall be locked and require re-authentication before the display is accessible. The timeout duration shall be configurable by the system administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 requires compliance with DHS IT security directives, which typically mandate session timeout policies for internal operational tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable as a standalone Windows client application on a standard exercise operations center PC, with no dependency on server-side rendering infrastructure or cloud services for core display functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 constrains deployment to a client PC; cloud or server-side rendering dependencies are explicitly out of scope.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each 60-second data refresh cycle — including data retrieval from both source systems, variance recalculation, color-code re-rendering, and full display update — within 10 seconds of the scheduled refresh trigger, such that the display is visually updated and current within 70 seconds of the previous refresh under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 requires no perceptible degradation during the 60-second refresh cycle; a 10-second processing budget ensures the display remains operationally current.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve 99% availability during all scheduled exercise operation windows, including exercises conducted outside standard business hours (evenings, weekends, and multi-day events). Planned maintenance windows shall be scheduled outside of exercise calendar windows and shall be coordinated with the IT Administrator and Exercise Director in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-01 defines a 99% availability SLA; the domain expert advisory notes that exercises frequently run outside standard business hours, requiring the SLA to cover the full exercise schedule window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render the graphical timeline and data table display without perceptible lag (frame update latency not to exceed 500 milliseconds) during normal user interactions including filter application, full exercise view activation, time segment navigation, and pause/resume operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: High-tempo exercise execution requires immediate visual response to controller interactions; sub-500ms UI responsiveness prevents decision-making delays.

generated_requirements.text: In Projection Mode, all primary Event and Activity data fields displayed in the data table and graphical timeline shall be legible by 90% of viewers at a viewing distance of 30 feet from a 10×10 projection screen. Color-coded status indicators shall be distinguishable at a viewing distance of up to 100 feet from the same screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 and 8 define specific readability KPIs at 30-foot and 100-foot distances that must be met for the projection use case.

generated_requirements.text: 90% of new users with no prior EEMDS training shall be able to independently initiate an Event or Activity display, apply a venue or key event filter, and navigate to a source application record via deep link within 90 minutes of first use.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 defines a measurable learnability KPI of 90% task completion within 90 minutes without formal training.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate users against the organization's designated identity provider (e.g., Active Directory or equivalent DHS-approved directory service) rather than maintaining a locally managed credential store, in compliance with applicable DHS IT security policy.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 and DHS IT security directives typically require integration with an approved identity provider rather than local credential management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce zero unauthorized access incidents. Access control logic shall correctly distinguish authorized from unauthorized users in 100% of authentication attempts, with no false-positive grants of access.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 7 defines a zero-tolerance KPI for unauthorized access, requiring 100% accuracy in access control enforcement.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between EEMDS and the source systems (Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0) shall be transmitted over encrypted channels appropriate to the classification level of the MSEL data being handled (at minimum, transport-layer encryption for Sensitive But Unclassified / CUI data). EEMDS shall not cache or persist MSEL data to unencrypted local storage.
generated_requirements.rationale: MSEL data in DHS exercises is frequently SBU/CUI; data in transit and at rest must be protected in accordance with applicable classification handling requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The history and audit log shall be stored in a tamper-evident format such that any unauthorized modification or deletion of log records is detectable. The log shall be retained for a minimum period defined by DHS records retention policy and shall be accessible only to authorized users with the AAR Analyst or Administrator role.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit log integrity is essential for post-exercise AAR validity and DHS compliance; tamper-evidence and access restriction prevent log manipulation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be maintainable such that updates to the DHS color schema specification can be applied to all affected UI elements by modifying a single centralized configuration or theme file, without requiring changes to application source code for individual UI components.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS color schema is an externally governed standard subject to change; centralized theme management minimizes the cost and risk of compliance updates.

generated_requirements.text: The integration layer connecting EEMDS to each source system shall be implemented as a modular, independently configurable component such that a change to the interface of one source system (e.g., a version upgrade of Sync Matrix 1.0) requires changes only to that source system's integration module and does not affect the integration with the other source system or the core display logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Source system interface changes are a known risk; modular integration architecture isolates change impact and reduces maintenance cost.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any Windows client PC meeting the minimum hardware and OS specifications defined in the deployment guide, without requiring custom hardware, specialized drivers, or modifications to the base OS configuration beyond standard application installation.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 requires deployment on a standard client PC; portability across standard Windows configurations ensures operational flexibility across exercise venues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support simultaneous connection to and data retrieval from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 within a single refresh cycle, presenting their data in a unified display without requiring the user to switch between application contexts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 5 requires consolidation of both source systems into a single display; simultaneous retrieval within one cycle ensures data currency parity between sources.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all user authentication events (successful logins, failed login attempts, session timeouts, and logouts) with timestamps in both local and exercise time, user identifier, and client machine identifier. These authentication logs shall be stored separately from the Event history report and shall be accessible to the system administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS IT security policy and the zero-unauthorized-access objective require a complete authentication audit trail for security incident investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system's terminology, data field labels, and reporting constructs (e.g., MSEL, inject, venue, exercise objective) shall be consistent with Homeland Security Exercise and Evaluation Program (HSEEP) doctrine to ensure compatibility with HSEEP-standard AAR processes and reporting.
generated_requirements.rationale: HSEEP alignment ensures that EEMDS outputs are directly usable in standard DHS after-action review processes without translation or reformatting.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a mean time to recover (MTTR) of no more than 30 minutes following an unplanned failure during an exercise operation window, with documented recovery procedures available to the IT Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: High-tempo exercise execution cannot tolerate extended outages; a 30-minute MTTR bound supports the 99% availability objective and limits exercise disruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a display configuration interface that allows authorized users (Exercise Planner role) to modify display settings — including time segment definitions, active rendering profile, and filter presets — and save named configuration profiles that can be loaded at the start of each exercise.
generated_requirements.rationale: Exercise planners require pre-exercise configuration capability; named profiles support repeatable setup across multiple exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a graphical timeline in the left two-thirds display region that plots Events and Activities as nodes positioned according to their scheduled exercise time within the configured time segments, with each node color-coded according to the DHS-compliant variance color scheme.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD future state describes a graphical timeline with color-coded Event nodes as the primary situational awareness visualization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the current data staleness age (time elapsed since last successful refresh) as a continuously updating counter visible in both Projection Mode and Workstation Mode, independent of the paused/active state of the refresh cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Continuous staleness visibility ensures controllers always know the age of displayed data, which is critical for decision-making confidence during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide role-based access differentiation between at minimum the following roles: (a) Exercise Controller — full display interaction including filter, pause/resume, and deep-link navigation; (b) Exercise Planner — all Controller capabilities plus display configuration and time segment management; (c) AAR Analyst — read-only access to history reports and variance charts; (d) System Administrator — user access management and system configuration. Role assignments shall be managed by the System Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple stakeholder roles with distinct capability needs require role-based access differentiation to enforce least-privilege access and protect configuration integrity.

domain: emergency management / homeland security exercise operations
sme_subject: DHS exercise management and operations center systems specialist
sme_advisory: EEMDS is a read-only situational awareness display tool operating in a high-tempo, time-critical DHS exercise environment where display failures or data staleness directly degrade exercise fidelity and controller decision-making. The requirements engineer must pay close attention to the interplay between exercise time vs. wall-clock time, the strict DHS color schema compliance regime, and the dual-source integration architecture — all of which introduce subtle but consequential failure modes that a generalist might underspecify. Special attention should be given to graceful degradation behaviors (e.g., what the display shows when a source system is unreachable), projection-environment usability constraints, and the auditability chain from source-system changes through to the EEMDS history report, as these are the areas most likely to produce post-exercise AAR failures or compliance findings.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall refresh the display every 60 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The application shall match the color of the schema set forth by Department of Homeland Security
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be readable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to read Event / Activity data from a viewing distance of 30
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available during normal business hours. As long as the user has access to the client PC the system will be available 99% of the time during the first six months of operation.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: If projected the data must be understandable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to determine that Events or Activities are occuring in current time from a viewing distance of 100
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall ensure that it can only be accessed by authorized users. The product will be able to distinguish between authorized and unauthorized users in all access attempts
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be intuitive and self-explanatory. : 90% of new users shall be able to start the display of Events or Activities within 90 minutes of using the product.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall respond fast to keep up-to-date data in the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a MDI form that allows for the viewing of the graph and the data table.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events in a vertical table by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the Events in a graph by time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display Events or Activities.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The display shall have two regions: left 2/3 of the display is graphical right 1/3 of the display is a data table
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The data displayed in both the nodes within the graph and the rows in the table are MSEL Summary data
ground_truth_requirements.label: NONE

ground_truth_requirements.text: The table side of the display shall be split into 2 regions: sequential and temporal.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The top 1/4 of the table will hold events that are to occur sequentially.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The bottom 3/4 of the table will hold events that occur according to its relevance to current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall color code events according to their variance from current time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display data from the Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 applications
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall link Events back to either the Sync Matrix 1.0 or the Exercise Managment Tool 1.0 applications for modifications.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow modification of the display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer a display of all the Events in the exercise.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall filter data by: Venues and Key Events.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a user to define the time segments
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display the local and exercise time in separate clocks
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall offer the ability to pause and resume the refresh of data.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide charts for the Activity or Event actual versus assumed time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall provide a history report of changes made to the Activity or Event data
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require every user to authenticate with valid registered-realtor credentials (username and password) before granting access to any system function or data.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and the security success criterion mandate that 100% of users are authenticated and authorized before accessing any system function.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce per-user MLS access scope, restricting each realtor's ability to query and modify only those MLS listings within the geographic regions and property-type categories covered by their MLS membership tier.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real estate license law and MLS board rules prohibit realtors from accessing listings outside their authorized membership scope.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access to any unregistered individual at all entry points, returning a clear, non-technical rejection message without revealing system internals.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 requires that unregistered individuals are denied access at all entry points to protect proprietary MLS and client data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support remote invalidation of a realtor's cached authentication credentials from the server side, rendering the local session token invalid within one synchronization cycle of the invalidation event.
generated_requirements.rationale: Brokerage data governance requires the ability to revoke access for lost/stolen devices or departing realtors without physical device access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall define a maximum offline session token validity period of 24 hours; if a cached token expires while the device is offline, the user shall be required to re-authenticate upon the next available network connection before regaining write access, while retaining read-only access to cached data until reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Security tokens issued at login will expire during extended offline periods, requiring a defined policy to balance security with field usability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to search MLS property listings by specifying one or more criteria (e.g., location, price range, property type, number of bedrooms/bathrooms), and shall display results including at least one property image per listing.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS Query and Search is a core in-scope capability required to eliminate the field productivity gap described in the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS property search results within 30 seconds of query submission under normal operating conditions, measured from the moment the user submits the search to the moment results are rendered on screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD defines a hard performance ceiling of 30 seconds for search results as a measurable business success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: Every screen on which MLS-sourced listing data is displayed shall include mandatory attribution comprising the listing broker's name, the MLS logo, and a data freshness timestamp indicating when the data was last retrieved or synchronized, in compliance with IDX/VOW display rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS board IDX/VOW policies (e.g., NAR MLS Policy Statement 7.58) require mandatory attribution on every screen where listings appear to avoid MLS access revocation.

generated_requirements.text: All property listing data displayed or cached on-device shall use field names and data types conforming to the RESO Data Dictionary standard, ensuring interoperability with the MLS API and consistency in CMA report generation and search result rendering.
generated_requirements.rationale: RESO Data Dictionary compliance is required for MLS board conformance and ensures consistent data interpretation across search, CMA, and offline views.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to create new property listings in the MLS from the mobile device, enforcing data validation rules on all required fields before submission to prevent malformed or incorrect data from entering the MLS.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 mandates data validation on all listing creation operations to maintain MLS data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to update existing MLS property listings from the mobile device, enforcing the same data validation rules as listing creation before submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS Listing Management is an in-scope capability, and BR-02 requires validation on all update operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall create an immutable audit log entry for every MLS listing creation and update operation, recording the realtor's identity, timestamp, the field(s) modified, original values, and new values, and shall retain these logs for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS board audit requirements mandate a traceable record of all write operations against listing data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save property search results to local device storage for later review, and saved results shall remain accessible in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 requires that realtors can save and later access search results, including when offline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report based on property criteria explicitly specified by the realtor, and shall deliver the completed report within 60 seconds of criteria submission, measured end-to-end from submission to display.
generated_requirements.rationale: CMA generation is a core in-scope capability, and the BRD defines a hard 60-second performance ceiling as a business success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not generate a CMA report unless the realtor has explicitly entered property criteria; the system shall prompt the user for required criteria if any mandatory fields are absent before initiating report generation.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 prohibits CMA generation without explicit criteria input to ensure report accuracy and prevent meaningless outputs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate CMA reports within 60 seconds of criteria entry under normal operating conditions, measured from criteria submission to full report rendering on the device screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD defines a hard performance ceiling of 60 seconds for CMA report generation as a measurable business success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map showing property locations for a specified area and shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions to any property selected by the realtor, available only for properties within the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 scopes mapping and directions to MLS-accessible properties, and the BRD identifies navigation overhead as a key pain point to eliminate.

generated_requirements.text: The driving directions feature shall define its rendering approach as a query to a licensed third-party mapping tile service, with the specific handoff protocol (native app call vs. in-app rendering) documented and the data licensing terms verified before implementation; map tiles shall not be cached locally beyond the session unless an offline caching license is obtained.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies that the directions handoff protocol has distinct data licensing, caching, and offline implications that must be explicitly resolved.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download, display, and allow management of client contacts and appointments on the mobile device, synchronized with the office system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contact and Appointment Management is a core in-scope capability required to eliminate data fragmentation identified in the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronize contact and appointment data with the office system once every hour without requiring any action from the realtor, transferring only changed records (delta synchronization) rather than full dataset dumps.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 mandates hourly automatic synchronization; delta sync is required to accommodate the low-bandwidth cellular connections typical of Windows CE and Palm OS devices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor immediately upon the next completed synchronization cycle when new appointments are discovered, and shall also notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to a system-initiated appointment request, without requiring the realtor to manually check for updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires automated appointment response notifications to reduce scheduling lag and improve agent responsiveness.

generated_requirements.text: Because Windows CE and Palm OS do not support modern push notification infrastructure (APNs/FCM), appointment response notifications shall be implemented via a polling mechanism; the system shall poll the server for appointment responses at a configurable interval not exceeding 15 minutes, and the polling process shall consume no more than 5% of device battery per hour under normal conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: The domain expert advisory identifies the absence of push notification infrastructure on target platforms, requiring a polling architecture with defined battery and data impact constraints.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate outbound calls to sellers or buyers for the purpose of scheduling appointments only to contacts associated with the authenticated realtor's client records, and all such communications must be traceable to the realtor's account.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 restricts system-initiated calls to the realtor's own client contacts and requires traceability.

generated_requirements.text: Before initiating any system-generated outbound call to a seller or buyer, the system shall verify that: (a) the contact has provided documented consent to receive automated calls, (b) the current local time at the called party's location is between 08:00 and 21:00, and (c) the contact's number does not appear on the applicable Do-Not-Call registry; the system shall block the call and log the reason if any condition is not met.
generated_requirements.rationale: TCPA compliance requires consent verification, calling-hours restrictions, and DNC list checking before any automated outbound call is placed.

generated_requirements.text: When internet connectivity is unavailable, the system shall operate in a read-only offline mode, allowing the realtor to view previously downloaded search results, CMA reports, contacts, and appointments without interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 mandates read-only offline access to previously downloaded content to eliminate the current total loss of access in low-connectivity areas.

generated_requirements.text: Write operations (listing updates, contact edits, new appointments) that cannot be completed while the device is offline shall be queued locally on the device and automatically submitted to the server upon reconnection, with the user notified of successful submission or any submission errors.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires that offline write operations are queued for submission upon reconnection to prevent data loss.

generated_requirements.text: When an offline-queued write operation conflicts with a server-side modification to the same record made during the offline period, the system shall apply a defined conflict resolution policy: server-side changes shall take precedence (server-wins) for MLS listing fields governed by MLS board rules; for contact and appointment records, the most recently timestamped change shall take precedence (last-write-wins); the realtor shall be notified of any resolved conflict with a summary of the discarded changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: The elicitation strategy identifies offline write-queue conflict resolution as underspecified in the BRD; a defined merge policy is required to prevent silent data loss or corruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall save and restore user preferences — including search filters, display settings, and saved search results — across sessions and system failures, such that 100% of saved preferences are restored to their last known state following any failure or connectivity interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and the data integrity success criterion require complete preference persistence and restoration.

generated_requirements.text: All data stored locally on the device — including cached MLS listings, client contact records, CMA reports, authentication tokens, and user preferences — shall be encrypted at rest using a minimum of 128-bit AES encryption, with encryption keys derived from the user's authentication credentials and not stored in plaintext on the device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Windows CE and Palm OS devices are highly susceptible to physical theft; the domain expert advisory requires encryption at rest with a specified standard and key management approach.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a remote wipe capability that, upon administrator instruction, deletes all locally cached MLS data, client contact records, authentication tokens, and user preferences from the device within one synchronization cycle of the wipe command being issued.
generated_requirements.rationale: Brokerage data governance obligations require remote wipe of sensitive data from lost, stolen, or decommissioned devices.

generated_requirements.text: MLS property images cached locally on the device shall include embedded attribution metadata (listing broker name, MLS source) and shall not be retained in local storage beyond 48 hours from the time of download, after which they must be re-fetched from the MLS upon next access, in compliance with MLS image use and copyright policies.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS image licensing policies govern how long images may be cached and require attribution; violations risk MLS access revocation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 24 hours per day, 365 days per year, achieving a minimum availability rate of 99.9%, defined as no more than 1 failed access attempt per 1,000 attempts, measured over any rolling 30-day period.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD defines 99.9% availability as a hard business success criterion to maximize agent productivity at all hours.

generated_requirements.text: The system backend shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous authenticated users at launch without degradation of the 30-second search and 60-second CMA performance ceilings.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD specifies 1,000 simultaneous users at launch as a hard capacity requirement tied to the current agent population.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be designed to scale to support 5,000 simultaneous authenticated users within 12 months of launch without requiring a fundamental redesign, achieved through horizontal scaling of backend services.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD projects a fivefold growth in the realtor population within 12 months and requires the architecture to accommodate this without redesign.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall run natively on devices operating Windows CE and Palm OS, conforming to the native UI standards, conventions, standard menus, and navigation buttons of each respective platform.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD constrains the platform to Windows CE and Palm OS, and requires native UI conformance on each target platform.

generated_requirements.text: The self-service installation process shall be completable by an untrained realtor without printed instructions, and at least 90% of untrained realtors in usability testing shall successfully complete installation on a first attempt without external assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD defines a hard KPI that 90% of untrained realtors must successfully self-install, eliminating training overhead and IT involvement.

generated_requirements.text: The application user interface shall use terminology, workflows, and navigation patterns natural to the real estate profession, such that a realtor with no formal training on the product can locate and use any core feature without consulting documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD requires the system to be usable by realtors with no formal training, necessitating a self-explanatory, domain-appropriate UI.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the mobile device and the server (synchronization, MLS queries, authentication) shall be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher, or the strongest equivalent protocol supported by the Windows CE and Palm OS platform SDKs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transmission of sensitive MLS data, client records, and authentication credentials over cellular or Wi-Fi networks requires encryption in transit to prevent interception.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based authorization such that only realtors with active, valid MLS membership status may perform MLS write operations (listing creation and update); realtors whose membership has lapsed shall be restricted to read-only MLS access until membership is reinstated.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS board rules require that only currently authorized members may modify listing data, necessitating membership-status-aware authorization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect the transition between online and offline states automatically and shall notify the realtor with a clear, non-intrusive status indicator when the device enters or exits offline mode, without requiring any manual action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors must be aware of their connectivity state to understand data currency and the limitations of offline mode.

generated_requirements.text: The hourly synchronization process shall complete within 5 minutes of its scheduled start time under normal network conditions, and shall log the outcome (success, partial success, or failure) with a timestamp accessible to the IT administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD requires contacts and appointments to be no more than 60 minutes stale; a bounded sync completion window and logging ensure this SLA is verifiable and failures are detectable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover automatically from transient synchronization failures (e.g., brief network interruption) by retrying the synchronization up to three times with exponential back-off before logging a failure and alerting the IT administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transient network failures are common on mobile devices; automatic retry prevents unnecessary sync failures and data staleness.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall preserve all locally cached data, queued offline write operations, and user preferences intact across unexpected application crashes or device restarts, with no data loss upon recovery.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 requires 100% restoration of saved preferences after any failure; this extends the principle to all locally stored data.

generated_requirements.text: The application codebase shall be structured with a clear separation between platform-specific UI layers (Windows CE and Palm OS) and shared business logic, such that a defect fix or feature change in shared logic requires modification in only one location.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting two legacy platforms simultaneously requires an architecture that minimizes duplicated code to reduce maintenance cost and defect risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all authentication attempts (successful and failed), including realtor identity, timestamp, device identifier, and outcome, and these logs shall be retained for a minimum of 90 days and accessible to the IT administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Security audit requirements and the zero-unauthorized-access success criterion require a complete, reviewable authentication audit trail.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a maximum of 5 consecutive failed authentication attempts per account, after which the account shall be temporarily locked for a minimum of 15 minutes, and the IT administrator shall be notified of the lockout event.
generated_requirements.rationale: Brute-force attack prevention is essential given that the device may be physically accessible to unauthorized individuals.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a data currency label on all offline-mode screens showing MLS listing data, indicating the date and time the data was last synchronized, to inform the realtor that the displayed information may not reflect the current MLS state.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS board display rules require data freshness labeling, and realtors must be informed when viewing potentially stale cached data to avoid acting on outdated pricing or availability.

generated_requirements.text: The local device storage used for cached MLS data, contacts, appointments, CMA reports, and user preferences shall not exceed 80% of the available storage capacity on the target device, and the application shall warn the realtor when storage utilization exceeds 70% of the allocated limit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Windows CE and Palm OS devices have severely constrained local storage; unmanaged cache growth can render the device inoperable and corrupt locally stored data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the IT administrator with a management interface to register new realtors, deactivate existing realtor accounts, assign or modify MLS access scope per realtor, and trigger remote credential invalidation or device wipe, without requiring physical access to the realtor's device.
generated_requirements.rationale: IT administration of user accounts, access scope, and device security is required to support organizational growth and enforce brokerage data governance policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an in-application help mechanism — such as contextual tooltips or a searchable FAQ — that uses real estate domain terminology and is accessible from every primary screen, enabling realtors to resolve common usage questions without contacting support or consulting external documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: The no-training-required constraint and the 90% self-installation KPI require that the application itself provides sufficient guidance for realtors to resolve questions independently.

sme_subject: MLS integration and mobile real estate platform architect
sme_advisory: This system sits at the intersection of MLS data governance, mobile offline-first architecture, and real estate industry compliance — a combination that generalist engineers frequently underestimate. The requirements engineer must pay close attention to MLS board rules (RESO standards, IDX/VOW policy) that govern how listing data may be displayed, cached, and transmitted on mobile devices, as violations can result in MLS access revocation. Given the Windows CE and Palm OS platform targets, the engineer should also probe deeply into platform-specific constraints around local data storage, cryptographic capability, background sync scheduling, and native UI conformance, since these legacy platforms impose hard limits that directly shape what is architecturally achievable for offline resilience, security, and scalability.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user interface shall have standard menus buttons for navigation

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall have a professional appearance

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be used by realtors with no training.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be intuitive and self explanatory.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be easy for a realtor to learn.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall use symbols and words that are naturally understandable by the realtor community.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The search results shall be returned no later 30 seconds after the user has entered the search criteria

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall generate a CMA report in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The CMA report shall be returned no later 60 seconds after the user has entered the CMA report criteria.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize contacts and appointments in an acceptable time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall synchronize with the office system every hour.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be available for use 24 hours per day 365 days per year
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Out of 1000 accesses to the system the system is available 999 times.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall operate in offline mode whenever internet connection is unavailable.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall allow the user to view previously downloaded search results CMA reports and appointments.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall retain user preferences in the event of a failure.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 100% of saved user preferences shall be restored when system comes back online.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able to support multiple remote users
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be able support 1000 simultaneous users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be capable of handling the existing 1000 users. This number is expected to grow 5 times within the next year.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall be installed by an untrained realtor without recourse to separately-printed instructions.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to install the product on their device without printed instructions
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product is expected to run on Windows CE and Palm operating systems.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only registered realtors shall be able to access the system.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Every user of the system shall be authenticated and authorized.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product shall prevent its data from incorrect data being introduced.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall allow a real estate agent to query MLS information
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to get driving directions to a selected property
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The user shall be able to download appointments and contact information for clients
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall display clear property images in the search results
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall update or create new property listings in the MLS
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Realtor shall be able to generate a CMA report based on property criteria

ground_truth_requirements.text: The system shall be able to call the seller or buyer to schedule an appointment

ground_truth_requirements.text: The realtor shall be notified of new client appointments after automatic synchronization with office system
